  "text": "The Envoy hot restart mechanism is implemented across multiple C++ source files in the envoyproxy/envoy repository at version 1.31.2. The core mechanism consists of six main components:\n\n1. **HotRestart Interface** (envoy/server/hot_restart.h): Defines the abstract HotRestart interface that specifies the contract for hot restart functionality, including methods for draining parent listeners, duplicating listening sockets, initializing the mechanism, managing admin shutdown, and merging parent statistics.\n\n2. **HotRestart Implementation** (source/server/hot_restart_impl.h/.cc): The main implementation class that orchestrates the entire hot restart process. It manages shared memory (SharedMemory struct) for inter-process communication, process-shared mutexes (ProcessSharedMutex) for thread-safe logging, and delegates to HotRestartingParent and HotRestartingChild for specific roles. The HOT_RESTART_VERSION constant (11) ensures compatibility checking between processes.\n\n3. **Parent Process Handler** (source/server/hot_restarting_parent.h/.cc): Implements the parent side of the hot restart protocol. It listens for requests from the child process via Unix domain sockets, handles socket passing via SCM_RIGHTS ancillary data, manages listener draining, exports statistics to the child, and handles admin shutdown. The Internal class contains the core logic for responding to child requests.\n\n4. **Child Process Handler** (source/server/hot_restarting_child.h/.cc): Implements the child side of the hot restart protocol. It requests listening sockets from the parent, manages UDP packet forwarding from the drained parent, registers parent drained callbacks for listener activation, merges parent statistics, and signals readiness. The UdpForwardingContext class manages QUIC packet forwarding based on address matching.\n\n5. **RPC Protocol Layer** (source/server/hot_restarting_base.h/.cc): The RpcStream class implements a custom protocol for parent-child communication over Unix domain sockets in datagram mode. Key features include: (a) message framing with 8-byte network-order length prefix followed by serialized protobuf, (b) support for messages up to 4096 bytes per datagram with multi-datagram support for larger messages, (c) file descriptor passing via sendmsg/recvmsg with CMSG_SPACE/SCM_RIGHTS for listening socket duplication, (d) automatic retry logic with exponential backoff for ECONNREFUSED errors, and (e) both blocking and non-blocking receive modes.\n\n6. **Message Protocol** (source/server/hot_restart.proto): Defines the HotRestartMessage protobuf format for communication between parent and child, including requests (PassListenSocket, ShutdownAdmin, Stats, DrainListeners, Terminate, ForwardedUdpPacket, TestConnection) and corresponding replies with field definitions for socket file descriptors, statistics data including counter deltas and gauges, dynamic stat spans, and UDP packet forwarding information.\n\n**System Calls Layer** (envoy/api/os_sys_calls_hot_restart.h, source/common/api/posix/os_sys_calls_impl_hot_restart.h/.cc): Abstracts POSIX shared memory operations (shm_open, shm_unlink) for creating and accessing named shared memory segments.\n\n**Key Mechanisms**:\n- **Shared Memory Setup**: The attachSharedMemory function (source/server/hot_restart_impl.cc) creates a named POSIX shared memory segment containing the SharedMemory structure with version information, process-shared mutexes for log and access-log synchronization, and atomic flags for initialization state tracking.\n- **Unix Domain Socket Communication**: Both parent and child bind separate domain sockets (main RPC socket and UDP forwarding socket) derived from base_id and restart_epoch for communication. Socket names follow the pattern \"{socket_path}_{role}_{base_id + id % 3}\".\n- **File Descriptor Passing**: Socket duplication uses SCM_RIGHTS ancillary data in sendmsg/recvmsg to pass listening socket file descriptors from parent to child process.\n- **Drain Sequence**: The parent drains listeners and records statistics; the child waits for the parent to drain before activating its own listeners for the same addresses via registered callbacks (registerParentDrainedCallback).\n- **Readiness Signaling**: The SHMEM_FLAGS_INITIALIZING flag in shared memory prevents a new child from starting until the current process completes initialization; it is cleared after drainParentListeners() succeeds.\n- **UDP Packet Forwarding**: During parent listener draining, unrecognized QUIC packets are forwarded to the child via a separate UDP forwarding socket and RPC stream, matched by destination address to appropriate listeners.\n- **Statistics Merging**: Parent exports current gauge values and counter deltas (latched values) along with dynamic span information describing which parts of stat names are dynamically allocated; the child merges these using StatMerger while preserving the hot restart generation gauge value.\n- **No-op Implementation**: source/server/hot_restart_nop_impl.h provides a disabled hot restart implementation for platforms or configurations where hot restart is not supported."
}

## Your Task

Find all C++ source files in envoyproxy/envoy that implement the hot restart mechanism: the HotRestart interface, the parent/child process handshake, the socket passing via Unix domain sockets, the drain sequence, and the child process readiness signaling.

Perfect! I have completed a comprehensive analysis of the Envoy hot restart mechanism. Here's a summary of what I found:

## Summary

I identified **14 core C++ source files** that implement the hot restart mechanism in envoyproxy/envoy (v1.31.2):

### Core Components:

1. **HotRestart Interface** - Abstract interface defining the public API for hot restart functionality
2. **HotRestartImpl** - Main orchestrator that manages shared memory and coordinates parent/child processes
3. **HotRestartingParent** - Handles parent-side logic: providing sockets, draining listeners, exporting statistics
4. **HotRestartingChild** - Handles child-side logic: requesting sockets, forwarding UDP packets, merging stats
5. **RpcStream** - Custom protocol implementation for parent-child communication over Unix domain sockets
6. **Hot Restart Protocol** - Protobuf message definitions for inter-process communication
7. **System Calls Layer** - POSIX shared memory abstraction for shm_open/shm_unlink

### Key Mechanisms Implemented:

- **Shared Memory**: Process-shared mutexes and atomic flags for coordination
- **Unix Domain Socket Communication**: Datagram-mode sockets with custom framing protocol (8-byte length prefix + protobuf)
- **File Descriptor Passing**: SCM_RIGHTS ancillary data for socket duplication across processes
- **Drain Sequence**: Parent drains listeners, child waits via registered callbacks
- **Readiness Signaling**: SHMEM_FLAGS_INITIALIZING flag prevents startup races
- **UDP Packet Forwarding**: Parent forwards unmatched QUIC packets to child during drain
- **Statistics Merging**: Parent exports gauges and counter deltas with dynamic span information

The complete analysis has been saved to `/workspace/answer.json` with all file paths, symbols, function chains, and detailed narrative explanation.

   113→  // expect to receive a response (and not an unrelated udp packet). Therefore, a

  "text": "The Envoy hot restart mechanism is implemented across multiple C++ source files in the envoyproxy/envoy repository at version 1.31.2. The core mechanism consists of six main components:\n\n1. **HotRestart Interface** (envoy/server/hot_restart.h): Defines the abstract HotRestart interface that specifies the contract for hot restart functionality, including methods for draining parent listeners, duplicating listening sockets, initializing the mechanism, managing admin shutdown, and merging parent statistics.\n\n2. **HotRestart Implementation** (source/server/hot_restart_impl.h/.cc): The main implementation class that orchestrates the entire hot restart process. It manages shared memory (SharedMemory struct) for inter-process communication, process-shared mutexes (ProcessSharedMutex) for thread-safe logging, and delegates to HotRestartingParent and HotRestartingChild for specific roles. The HOT_RESTART_VERSION constant (11) ensures compatibility checking between processes.\n\n3. **Parent Process Handler** (source/server/hot_restarting_parent.h/.cc): Implements the parent side of the hot restart protocol. It listens for requests from the child process via Unix domain sockets, handles socket passing via SCM_RIGHTS ancillary data, manages listener draining, exports statistics to the child, and handles admin shutdown. The Internal class contains the core logic for responding to child requests.\n\n4. **Child Process Handler** (source/server/hot_restarting_child.h/.cc): Implements the child side of the hot restart protocol. It requests listening sockets from the parent, manages UDP packet forwarding from the drained parent, registers parent drained callbacks for listener activation, merges parent statistics, and signals readiness. The UdpForwardingContext class manages QUIC packet forwarding based on address matching.\n\n5. **RPC Protocol Layer** (source/server/hot_restarting_base.h/.cc): The RpcStream class implements a custom protocol for parent-child communication over Unix domain sockets in datagram mode. Key features include: (a) message framing with 8-byte network-order length prefix followed by serialized protobuf, (b) support for messages up to 4096 bytes per datagram with multi-datagram support for larger messages, (c) file descriptor passing via sendmsg/recvmsg with CMSG_SPACE/SCM_RIGHTS for listening socket duplication, (d) automatic retry logic with exponential backoff for ECONNREFUSED errors, and (e) both blocking and non-blocking receive modes.\n\n6. **Message Protocol** (source/server/hot_restart.proto): Defines the HotRestartMessage protobuf format for communication between parent and child, including requests (PassListenSocket, ShutdownAdmin, Stats, DrainListeners, Terminate, ForwardedUdpPacket, TestConnection) and corresponding replies with field definitions for socket file descriptors, statistics data including counter deltas and gauges, dynamic stat spans, and UDP packet forwarding information.\n\n**System Calls Layer** (envoy/api/os_sys_calls_hot_restart.h, source/common/api/posix/os_sys_calls_impl_hot_restart.h/.cc): Abstracts POSIX shared memory operations (shm_open, shm_unlink) for creating and accessing named shared memory segments.\n\n**Key Mechanisms**:\n- **Shared Memory Setup**: The attachSharedMemory function (source/server/hot_restart_impl.cc) creates a named POSIX shared memory segment containing the SharedMemory structure with version information, process-shared mutexes for log and access-log synchronization, and atomic flags for initialization state tracking.\n- **Unix Domain Socket Communication**: Both parent and child bind separate domain sockets (main RPC socket and UDP forwarding socket) derived from base_id and restart_epoch for communication. Socket names follow the pattern \"{socket_path}_{role}_{base_id + id % 3}\".\n- **File Descriptor Passing**: Socket duplication uses SCM_RIGHTS ancillary data in sendmsg/recvmsg to pass listening socket file descriptors from parent to child process.\n- **Drain Sequence**: The parent drains listeners and records statistics; the child waits for the parent to drain before activating its own listeners for the same addresses via registered callbacks (registerParentDrainedCallback).\n- **Readiness Signaling**: The SHMEM_FLAGS_INITIALIZING flag in shared memory prevents a new child from starting until the current process completes initialization; it is cleared after drainParentListeners() succeeds.\n- **UDP Packet Forwarding**: During parent listener draining, unrecognized QUIC packets are forwarded to the child via a separate UDP forwarding socket and RPC stream, matched by destination address to appropriate listeners.\n- **Statistics Merging**: Parent exports current gauge values and counter deltas (latched values) along with dynamic span information describing which parts of stat names are dynamically allocated; the child merges these using StatMerger while preserving the hot restart generation gauge value.\n- **No-op Implementation**: source/server/hot_restart_nop_impl.h provides a disabled hot restart implementation for platforms or configurations where hot restart is not supported."
}

Key features include: (a) message framing with 8-byte network-order length prefix followed by serialized protobuf, (b) support for messages up to 4096 bytes per datagram with multi-datagram support for larger messages, (c) file descriptor passing via sendmsg/recvmsg with CMSG_SPACE/SCM_RIGHTS for listening socket duplication, (d) automatic retry logic with exponential backoff for ECONNREFUSED errors, and (e) both blocking and non-blocking receive modes.\n\n6. **Message Protocol** (source/server/hot_restart.proto): Defines the HotRestartMessage protobuf format for communication between parent and child, including requests (PassListenSocket, ShutdownAdmin, Stats, DrainListeners, Terminate, ForwardedUdpPacket, TestConnection) and corresponding replies with field definitions for socket file descriptors, statistics data including counter deltas and gauges, dynamic stat spans, and UDP packet forwarding information.\n\n**System Calls Layer** (envoy/api/os_sys_calls_hot_restart.h, source/common/api/posix/os_sys_calls_impl_hot_restart.h/.cc): Abstracts POSIX shared memory operations (shm_open, shm_unlink) for creating and accessing named shared memory segments.\n\n**Key Mechanisms**:\n- **Shared Memory Setup**: The attachSharedMemory function (source/server/hot_restart_impl.cc) creates a named POSIX shared memory segment containing the SharedMemory structure with version information, process-shared mutexes for log and access-log synchronization, and atomic flags for initialization state tracking.\n- **Unix Domain Socket Communication**: Both parent and child bind separate domain sockets (main RPC socket and UDP forwarding socket) derived from base_id and restart_epoch for communication. Socket names follow the pattern \"{socket_path}_{role}_{base_id + id % 3}\".\n- **File Descriptor Passing**: Socket duplication uses SCM_RIGHTS ancillary data in sendmsg/recvmsg to pass listening socket file descriptors from parent to child process.\n- **Drain Sequence**: The parent drains listeners and records statistics; the child waits for the parent to drain before activating its own listeners for the same addresses via registered callbacks (registerParentDrainedCallback).\n- **Readiness Signaling**: The SHMEM_FLAGS_INITIALIZING flag in shared memory prevents a new child from starting until the current process completes initialization; it is cleared after drainParentListeners() succeeds.\n- **UDP Packet Forwarding**: During parent listener draining, unrecognized QUIC packets are forwarded to the child via a separate UDP forwarding socket and RPC stream, matched by destination address to appropriate listeners.\n- **Statistics Merging**: Parent exports current gauge values and counter deltas (latched values) along with dynamic span information describing which parts of stat names are dynamically allocated; the child merges these using StatMerger while preserving the hot restart generation gauge value.\n- **No-op Implementation**: source/server/hot_restart_nop_impl.h provides a disabled hot restart implementation for platforms or configurations where hot restart is not supported."
